evidence: Investing in innovation to accelerate organic growth is a critical component of Emerson's value creation strategy. The Company is focused on key growth initiatives across its software, control and intelligent devices portfolio.
Total spending for R&D, engineering expense and customer-funded engineering and development was 8.1 percent of sales in 2025 and in 2024
Our businesses increasingly rely on artificial intelligence solutions to optimize our operations, improve customer experiences, and enhance our products and services.
These initiatives include disruptive measurement technologies, software-defined automation systems, self-optimizing asset software and sustainability solutions.
AspenTech is now a wholly owned subsidiary of the Company... AspenTech's results are now consolidated into the Control Systems Software segment

We anticipate that the risk of cybersecurity attacks will increase as artificial intelligence capabilities improve... with the possibility of additional vulnerabilities being introduced through our own use of artificial intelligence
Our artificial intelligence efforts subject us to risks related to accuracy, intellectual property infringement or misappropriation, data privacy, and cybersecurity, among others

The regulatory landscape surrounding artificial intelligence is rapidly evolving and the use of artificial intelligence may be subject to new legal or regulatory requirements, the impact of which may be prohibitive or pose further risks

limitations: ai_investment_intensity: No dollar amounts or quantified spend specifically tied to AI investment are disclosed in the excerpts; R&D spend of 8.1% of sales is not disaggregated to AI specifically. The $7.2B AspenTech acquisition is a software/AI-adjacent acquisition but is not framed as AI capex in the excerpts.

ai_narrative_concreteness: AI use cases are named (operations optimization, customer experience, product enhancement) and AspenTech is a named AI/software initiative, but no deployment metrics or quantified business results are provided; some language remains aspirational.
capex_mentions: No AI-specific capex dollar figures appear in the excerpts; total capital expenditures of $431M (2025) are disclosed but not attributed to AI.
